problem_classes.existing_method: Database index / hash map / primary key lookup.
problem_classes.input_contract: Exact entity identifier or unambiguous canonical key.
problem_classes.name: Exact identity lookup
problem_classes.output_contract: Single entity id or hard miss.
problem_classes.required_adapter: None beyond query-object to key extraction.
problem_classes.required_provenance: Key source and last indexed update.
problem_classes.why_not_scratch: A custom resolver only recreates commodity indexing and risks regressions.

problem_classes.input_contract: Partial or noisy structured attributes over a fixed canonical entity catalog.
problem_classes.mismatch: Libraries expect tabular records and need a thin adapter from query-cue objects.
problem_classes.name: Structured fuzzy entity resolution
problem_classes.online_offline_behavior: Offline training plus online candidate lookup.
problem_classes.output_contract: Ranked candidate entity ids with scores or unresolved.
problem_classes.required_adapter: Query cue to record dict conversion, thresholded unresolved wrapper, ranked-id handoff.
problem_classes.required_latency: Low-latency query-time ranking after model setup.
problem_classes.required_provenance: Per-field evidence and candidate source catalog.
problem_classes.uncertainty_representation: Match probability or ranked similarity score.
problem_classes.why_not_scratch: Matching, calibration, and blocking are mature ER problems with extensive prior art.

problem_classes.input_contract: Messy record against a fixed canonical entity table.
problem_classes.mismatch: No first-class action-layer abstention label; unresolved must be wrapped around zero/low-score outputs.
problem_classes.name: Canonical gazetteer matching
problem_classes.online_offline_behavior: Pre-index canonical set, then online search.
problem_classes.required_adapter: Optional abstention threshold and score normalization.
problem_classes.required_provenance: Returned candidate ids and match scores.
problem_classes.why_not_scratch: Gazetteer matching is directly supported upstream, including indexing and scored search.

problem_classes.existing_method: Splink blocking rules / dedupe fingerprinter / standard ER blocking.
problem_classes.input_contract: Large candidate table plus blocking policy.
problem_classes.mismatch: Context-generated blocking is not novel by itself and must be compared to ordinary blocking baselines.
problem_classes.name: Candidate blocking
problem_classes.output_contract: Reduced candidate set with measurable recall/reduction trade-off.
problem_classes.required_adapter: External policy chooses among upstream blocking rules and logs recall/reduction.
problem_classes.required_provenance: Blocking rule identity and exclusion statistics.
problem_classes.uncertainty_representation: Pair completeness, reduction ratio, candidate recall.
problem_classes.why_not_scratch: Blocking is already a standard ER design axis with established metrics and tooling.

problem_classes.input_contract: Time-ordered observations with misses, clutter, and uncertain identity.
problem_classes.mismatch: Native interfaces are tracking-centric, so structured symbolic observations require a representation adapter.
problem_classes.name: Streaming observation-to-entity association
problem_classes.required_adapter: Structured observation to Stone Soup measurement/state adapter.
problem_classes.required_provenance: Association hypothesis trace and missed-detection handling.
problem_classes.why_not_scratch: JPDA/MHT-style data association is a mature field with difficult corner cases.

problem_classes.existing_method: Stone Soup JPDA / JPDA with LBP / initiator + deleter stack.
problem_classes.input_contract: Ambiguous sequential observations under clutter or missed detections.
problem_classes.mismatch: Not a canonical tabular gazetteer matcher; best fit is sequential sensor-style association.
problem_classes.name: Multi-hypothesis identity tracking
problem_classes.output_contract: Competing identity/track hypotheses with confidence or delayed commitment.
problem_classes.required_adapter: Map symbolic events into tracking observations and keep native probabilities intact.
problem_classes.uncertainty_representation: Hypothesis probabilities and track existence evidence.
problem_classes.why_not_scratch: Maintaining calibrated competing hypotheses is exactly what tracking libraries already solve.

problem_classes.existing_method: Soar episodic memory / ACT-R declarative retrieval.
problem_classes.input_contract: Partial cue requesting a past episode or memory trace.
problem_classes.mismatch: Whole architectures are much larger than the current seam and do not natively satisfy typed external authority contracts.
problem_classes.name: Cue-based episodic retrieval
problem_classes.required_adapter: Adopt retrieval-pattern ideas, not full architecture runtime.
problem_classes.required_provenance: Episode identity, cue features, retrieval threshold.
problem_classes.why_not_scratch: Cue-based episodic retrieval and thresholded recall are classic cognitive-memory mechanisms.

problem_classes.existing_method: ACT-R spreading activation and partial matching; Soar semantic/episodic cueing.
problem_classes.input_contract: Cue plus active context or spreading-activation signal.
problem_classes.mismatch: Patterns exist, but not as a drop-in Python component for mixed ER/tracking pipelines.
problem_classes.name: Context-biased declarative retrieval
problem_classes.output_contract: Ranked retrieval candidates or thresholded miss.
problem_classes.required_adapter: External context must remain explicit and comparable against standard blocking/retrieval baselines.
problem_classes.uncertainty_representation: Activation, match score, or retrieval threshold.
problem_classes.why_not_scratch: Biased declarative retrieval is established; novelty cannot rest on 'adding context' alone.

problem_classes.existing_method: Indexed event store + explicit reducer + optional tracker outputs.
problem_classes.input_contract: Observed event log plus association outputs.
problem_classes.mismatch: No single audited library owns both symbolic event provenance and downstream action authority.
problem_classes.name: Temporal belief projection
problem_classes.output_contract: Current indexed belief state with support/conflict references.
problem_classes.required_adapter: Reducer that preserves support/conflict references instead of inventing new memory math.
problem_classes.required_provenance: Supporting episode ids and supersession links.
problem_classes.uncertainty_representation: Confidence, stale flags, conflict sets, possible-unobserved-change markers.
problem_classes.why_not_scratch: This is integration glue around already-understood indexing and tracking patterns, not a new matcher.

problem_classes.existing_method: Thresholded retrieval and unresolved/no-match outputs exist across ER and cognitive-memory systems.
problem_classes.input_contract: Native matcher/tracker outputs plus downstream action stakes.
problem_classes.mismatch: Authority boundary across heterogeneous native systems is integration-specific, but not enough to justify a new matcher.
problem_classes.name: Uncertainty-preserving commit authority
problem_classes.online_offline_behavior: Online gating between inference and action.
problem_classes.output_contract: Commit, abstain, or defer without erasing uncertainty.
problem_classes.required_adapter: Normalize native confidence surfaces into commit/defer semantics while keeping source evidence.
problem_classes.required_provenance: Why action was accepted, deferred, or rejected.
problem_classes.uncertainty_representation: Thresholded confidence, unresolved state, conflict trace.
problem_classes.why_not_scratch: The seam is decision policy and provenance retention, not reinvention of confidence scoring.

problem_classes.existing_method: Partial coverage via Dedupe no-match behavior and Stone Soup track initiation.
problem_classes.input_contract: Novel or unresolved observations that may refer to unseen entities.
problem_classes.mismatch: Full provisional-identity lifecycle crosses ER, tracking, and memory governance and is outside current benchmark scope.
problem_classes.name: Open-world new-entity lifecycle
problem_classes.output_contract: Deferred no-match, provisional identity, or new-track initiation.
problem_classes.required_adapter: Defer entirely until a narrower claim is defined.
problem_classes.required_latency: Online-safe deferral, slower offline consolidation acceptable.
problem_classes.required_provenance: Why a new entity was or was not created.
problem_classes.uncertainty_representation: No-match probability, provisional state, merge risk.
problem_classes.why_not_scratch: It is a large product/runtime problem, not the next minimal research seam.

problem_classes.existing_method: Composable but not closed by a single upstream system.
failure_modes: budget bias causing silent wrong commit
problem_classes.input_contract: Query/observation context plus a menu of native resolvers and blocking policies.
problem_classes.mismatch: Audited libraries expose native matchers or trackers, but none provides a shared external context/routing/budget interface with authority-preserving fallback across heterogeneous mechanisms.
problem_classes.name: Cross-mechanism routing and budget allocation
problem_classes.output_contract: Chosen native mechanism, candidate budget, fallback path, and abstention trace.
problem_classes.required_adapter: One small controller around adopted systems, tested against standard blocking and fallback baselines.
problem_classes.required_latency: Must save compute without hiding uncertainty.
problem_classes.required_provenance: Which mechanism ran, which candidates were filtered, and why fallback occurred.
problem_classes.uncertainty_representation: Coverage-risk-compute trade-off plus preserved native uncertainty.
problem_classes.why_not_scratch: Only the controller seam is still plausibly novel; rebuilding matchers would duplicate prior art.

requirement_matrix.mismatch: Need thin query-object adapter and abstention wrapper.
requirement_matrix.why_not_scratch: Would re-implement fuzzy matching and calibration.

requirement_matrix.mismatch: Must benchmark against ordinary blocking; otherwise novelty collapses.
requirement_matrix.required_adapter: External policy picks among upstream blocking policies.

requirement_matrix.existing_method: Splink probabilities / dedupe confidence / Stone Soup hypothesis weights
requirement_matrix.mismatch: Need shared uncertainty handoff to downstream action policy.
Requires observation adapter from symbolic events.
requirement_matrix.why_not_scratch: Sequential association is established tracking prior art.

requirement_matrix.existing_method: Tracking missed detections plus explicit belief reducer
requirement_matrix.why_not_scratch: This is reducer logic, not a new retrieval algorithm.

requirement_matrix.existing_method: Stone Soup initiators / unresolved ER no-match
requirement_matrix.mismatch: Full provisional identity lifecycle remains out of scope.
requirement_matrix.required_adapter: Defer until separate lifecycle benchmark exists.
requirement_matrix.why_not_scratch: Too large and underspecified for current stage.

requirement_matrix.existing_method: Partial overlap from MHT and unresolved ER
requirement_matrix.mismatch: No audited system gives the exact cross-memory authority contract for provisional identities.
requirement_matrix.why_not_scratch: Would balloon into a lifecycle architecture project.

requirement_matrix.mismatch: Requires authority policy spanning matcher and action layers.

requirement_matrix.existing_method: Native scores plus explicit controller logs
requirement_matrix.mismatch: No single library logs cross-mechanism decision provenance end to end.
requirement_matrix.why_not_scratch: Provenance is glue around adopted tools.

requirement_matrix.existing_method: Unresolved/no-match thresholds and deferred track initiation
requirement_matrix.mismatch: Need explicit authority boundary, not just low score.
requirement_matrix.why_not_scratch: Non-commit policy should wrap native scores, not replace them.

requirement_matrix.existing_method: Controller layer over native matcher/tracker outputs
requirement_matrix.mismatch: No upstream package owns context routing, budget control, and safe fallback across heterogeneous systems.
requirement_matrix.required_adapter: Small external controller with explicit ablations.
requirement_matrix.why_not_scratch: This is the only plausible minimal seam left after prior-art overlap.
